channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal coastal groups object to SA Lithium Highbury Mine expansion citing legal flaws and environmental risks
item.description: A coalition of KwaZulu-Natal coastal community organisations has lodged a formal objection against SA Lithium's proposed expansion of its Highbury open-cast lithium mine near Umzumbe on the South Coas...

item.title: Aleema Khan files petition challenging Imran Khan’s solitary confinement in Adiala Jail
item.description: Aleema Khan, sister of former PM and PTI chief Imran Khan, has filed a writ petition in the Islamabad High Court contesting the prolonged solitary confinement of her brother in Adiala Jail, Rawalpindi...

item.title: Evaluating ASIO's Approach to Chinese Political Interference in Australia
item.description: The Australian Security Intelligence Organisation (ASIO) has recently come under scrutiny due to its operational priorities and handling of national security threats. Following high-profile incidents ...

item.title: Transport Canada recalls over 20,000 Jeep Cherokees for potential rollaway issue
item.description: Transport Canada has issued a recall affecting more than 20,000 Jeep Cherokee SUVs due to a defect in the power transfer unit (PTU) that could cause vehicles to move unexpectedly, even when parked. Th...

item.title: Delhi High Court Judge Initiates Contempt Proceedings Over Defamatory Posts in Excise Policy Case
item.description: On Thursday, Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma of the Delhi High Court announced that she would initiate contempt proceedings against certain individuals previously discharged in the excise policy case, du...

item.title: Bette Davis Designed Her Own Iconic Makeup for Baby Jane
item.description: In the 1962 film “What Ever Happened to Baby Jane?”, Bette Davis personally created and applied her own makeup to achieve the grotesque appearance required for her character, Jane Hudson. Davis insist...

item.title: Gauteng Finance MEC Outlines Plans to Address Provincial and Municipal Debt
item.description: Gauteng’s Finance MEC, Nkululeko Dunga, has raised alarm over the escalating intergovernmental debt within the province, which he says poses serious structural and fiscal risks. As of March 2026, the ...

item.title: King makes a solidarity visit to Golders Green's Jewish community
item.description: The King recently visited Golders Green, a neighbourhood in London known for its significant Jewish population, to express his solidarity with the local community. The visit included meetings with com...

item.title: How Laurel Coppock’s Toyota Commercial Boosted Her Career and Net Worth
item.description: Laurel Coppock, born on 22 August 1970 in Boston, Massachusetts, is an American actress and comedian who rose to fame through her role in Toyota commercials. Before the ads, she had minor roles in pop...

item.title: Federal government introduces stricter compliance on school disability funding
item.description: The federal government has announced measures to tighten oversight of disability support funding for Australian school students, aiming to save approximately $463 million over four years. Schools curr...

item.title: Common Cancers in Nigeria and How to Spot Them Early
item.description: Cancer don dey increase well well for Nigeria and e don touch both men and women. For women, breast cancer and cervical cancer dey top, while for men, na prostate and liver cancer dey common. One big ...

item.title: Wes Streeting resigns as Health Secretary amid Labour leadership uncertainty
item.description: Wes Streeting has resigned as Health Secretary, escalating the ongoing leadership crisis within the Labour Party. In his resignation letter, Streeting criticised Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer for a ...

item.title: The Daily Wire faces layoffs amid challenges in right-wing media landscape
item.description: Over the past year, The Daily Wire, co-founded by American commentator Ben Shapiro in 2015, has experienced significant setbacks. The company recently announced another round of staff layoffs, followi...

item.title: Wall Street poised for gains as Trump and Xi meet for summit discussions
item.description: Investors on Wall Street were preparing for gains ahead of the opening bell as leaders from the United States and China, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping, met for a highly anticipated summit. The meeting h...

item.title: Senate Lockdown on May 13, 2026: Shooting Incident Timeline and Investigation
item.description: On May 13, 2026, a shooting incident occurred at the Philippine Senate, prompting a lockdown. The incident began at 5 p.m. with a request to secure the GSIS complex in Pasay City, where the Senate off...

item.title: President Ruto Leads Early 2026 Polls Amidst Close Contest with Kalonzo, Gachagua and Sifuna
item.description: A recent TIFA Research poll on the 2026 presidential race in Kenya shows President William Ruto leading with 24% support, maintaining a slight edge over his rivals. Trailing behind are Wiper Party lea...

item.title: Canada plans AI data centres under domestic control to strengthen data sovereignty
item.description: Canada is moving to develop AI data centres that are not just physically located within the country but also controlled under Canadian governance, aiming to reduce reliance on U.S. tech companies and ...

item.title: Tensions rise between Iran and UAE during BRICS foreign ministers meet in New Delhi
item.description: During the BRICS foreign ministers' meeting in New Delhi, Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi openly accused the UAE of being directly involved in military actions against Iran, according to Irania...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Removes Social Development Minister Sisisi Tolashe Amid Controversy
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has officially removed Sisisi Tolashe from her position as Social Development Minister, citing violations under section 91(2) of the Constitution. In her place, Sindisiwe Chi...

item.title: PTI Leaders Denied Court-Approved Meetings with Imran Khan at Adiala Jail
item.description: ISLAMABAD: On Thursday, PTI leaders faced another setback as their scheduled meeting with former Prime Minister and party founder Imran Khan at Adiala Jail did not take place, despite a clear Islamaba...
